title: Punniyarajan is not merely Aputhiran returned
source_observation: The poem connects the king to Aputhiran through rebirth while also giving him present parents kingdom relationships and duties
risk: Same-person shorthand could erase present identity and imply an unchanged permanent soul
decision: Portray causal continuity and ethical inheritance without treating the king as Aputhiran occupying another body
rationale: Protects Buddhist causation and the reality of the present life
required_review: Classical Tamil; Buddhist studies; performance; casting

title: Rebirth recognition is ethical revelation not personality replacement
source_observation: Reported information precedes the Peedihai's disclosure and remembered feeding experience
risk: A flashback device could instantly replace present personality with complete prior-life memory
decision: Stage recognition in layers and preserve the king's current knowledge habits and relationships after disclosure
rationale: Makes revelation transformative without flattening character
required_review: Buddhist studies; classical Tamil; screenplay; VFX; performance

title: Present kingship remains a moral responsibility equal to personal liberation
source_observation: Janamithran warns that subjects and creatures may suffer if the king leaves for tapas or moksha
risk: Renunciation could be presented as automatically superior to governance
decision: Keep living subjects inside the king's moral frame and make return to rule necessary to the arc
rationale: Preserves role-specific duty and prevents spiritualised abandonment
required_review: Kingship history; Buddhist ethics; screenplay; politics

title: Prosperity after his birth is symbolic evidence not proof of holiness
source_observation: The minister links rainfall abundance and absence of hunger with Punniyarajan's birth
risk: The film could present magical weather causation or imply prosperity requires a sacred ruler rather than material systems
decision: Preserve the poem's royal symbolism while showing cultivation storage labour and administration as separate causal layers
rationale: Maintains textual meaning without false historical or institutional certainty
required_review: Classical Tamil; environmental history; political history; production design

title: Janamithran receives serious ethical authority
source_observation: The minister names mothers children creatures and the king's duty to protect life
risk: He could become comic bureaucracy obstructing a spiritually enlightened ruler
decision: Give him courage intellectual coherence and continuing standing to ask what the revelation means for government
rationale: Strengthens the kingship conflict and represents subjects absent from the palace
required_review: Classical Tamil; performance; screenplay; political history

title: Manipallavam journey is a time-limited pilgrimage not unexamined abandonment
source_observation: The king says he will return in one month and delegates protection before commissioning a boat
risk: The screenplay could turn the journey into permanent abdication or consequence-free spiritual tourism
decision: Track the one-month limit delegation travel time and risks during absence and require return
rationale: Preserves the textual compromise between inquiry and duty
required_review: Classical Tamil; maritime history; kingship; continuity

title: Memory of Amudhasurabhi concerns service not ownership
source_observation: The king remembers receiving the vessel and feeding hungry people while the vessel is now in Manimekalai's hands
risk: Previous possession could be treated as a present claim to the sacred object or its moral prestige
decision: Let the memory restore obligation while Manimekalai retains custody and independent vocation
rationale: Protects both characters and the vessel's service logic
required_review: Buddhist studies; screenplay; props continuity; performance

title: Nine merchants complicate Aputhiran's self-relinquishment
source_observation: Deepathilahai shows that merchants returned found Aputhiran dead and also died on Manipallavam
risk: Their deaths could be beautified as loyal sacrifice or omitted to preserve a flawless saint narrative
decision: Use the revelation to expose unintended consequence without horror spectacle or assigning simple legal guilt
rationale: Prevents self-destruction from becoming a pure moral victory
required_review: Classical Tamil; Buddhist ethics; safeguarding; direction; editor

title: Return to rule completes the present arc
source_observation: Later narrative states that Punniyarajan returns to his country and continues ruling
risk: Return could feel like retreat from enlightenment unless governance becomes the site of ethical change
decision: End the branch with renewed public responsibility and develop any reforms as separately logged interpretations
rationale: Joins previous-life compassion to present institutional duty
required_review: Classical Tamil; kingship history; screenplay architecture; policy-history review

title: Same actor as Aputhiran remains unresolved
source_observation: Visual continuity could clarify rebirth but can also imply an unchanged soul or erase distinct upbringing
risk: Casting choice may predetermine the film's philosophy before the screenplay earns it
decision: Test same-actor related-actor and distinct-actor approaches before locking casting or VFX
rationale: Preserves doctrinal ambiguity and present-life individuality
required_review: Buddhist studies; casting; VFX; audience testing
